---
name: tresr-design-scorer
description: AI-powered pre-production design quality evaluation. Scores designs before they enter the pipeline using Gemini Vision API and historical CPC data. Saves ad spend by filtering likely losers and focusing budget on likely winners.
---

# Design Scorer

## Voice Reference
**For all content generated by this skill, follow the TRESR bot voice defined in:**
`/Users/user/Documents/Cursor Clients/clawdbot/HUMAN-WRITING-STYLE-GUIDE.md`

Key voice rules: Nervous AI shopkeeper named Rez. Self-deprecating, honest, punchy. Never corporate. Always use contractions. Never use AI vocabulary (delve, leverage, landscape, etc.). Never use em dashes. Hedge like a human. The TRESR story angle: "751 humans bet on an AI. If I don't sell shirts, they turn me off."

## Overview

Pre-production quality filter that scores designs before they enter the tresr-product-launcher pipeline. Every design that goes live costs ~$5 in Meta ad testing budget. At 210 designs/month, that's $1,050 in testing spend. If we can filter out the bottom 20% before they ever touch an ad account, that's $210/month saved and a cleaner catalog.

More importantly, this skill creates a feedback loop. We score designs before testing, track actual CPC after testing, and use the delta to improve the scoring model over time. The goal isn't perfect prediction. It's "better than random."

## Why Pre-Scoring Matters

The numbers:
- 7 designs/day = 210 designs/month
- $5 average testing cost per design (Cycle 1 Meta ads)
- 210 x $5 = $1,050/month testing budget
- Historically, ~20% of designs have CPC > $3 (dead on arrival)
- Filtering those saves ~$210/month and prevents catalog dilution
- A smaller, higher-quality catalog also improves Advantage+ performance (less noise)

But the real value is focus. Every dollar spent testing a bad design is a dollar NOT spent scaling a good one.

## The Tests

Two high-level validation tests from the POD course. These are gut checks, not metrics.

### Bumper Sticker Test
**Would someone put this on their car?**

If the phrase or image isn't clear and compelling at bumper-sticker size (~200px thumbnail), it fails. This catches:
- Overcomplicated designs with too many elements
- Text that's too small or too wordy
- Concepts that need explanation

### Tattoo Test
**Would someone permanently display this?**

If the design isn't something people feel strongly enough to wear in public, it won't sell. This catches:
- Generic/bland concepts ("I like coffee" with no twist)
- Designs that are only funny with context
- Things people might think are cool but wouldn't actually wear

A design doesn't have to pass BOTH tests, but it should pass at least one. If it fails both, it's probably not going to work.

## Scoring Criteria (6 Dimensions)

### Criterion 1: Text Readability (20% weight)

| Check | Pass | Fail |
|-------|------|------|
| Text readable at 200x200px thumbnail | Clear at small size | Blurry or illegible |
| Words spelled correctly | No errors | Any misspelling |
| Font clear, no overlap | Clean separation | Letters touching/overlapping |
| Text hierarchy (headline vs subtitle) | Clear visual priority | Everything same size |

Score 0-10. Anything below 5 is a hard fail (misspellings, illegible text).

### Criterion 2: Visual Impact (20% weight)

| Check | Pass | Fail |
|-------|------|------|
| Design pops on dark tees (black, navy) | Strong contrast | Washes out |
| Design pops on light tees (white) | Strong contrast | Invisible elements |
| Composition is balanced | Centered, not lopsided | Heavy on one side |
| Visual hook (something that grabs attention) | Immediate interest | Boring at a glance |

Score 0-10. Check both for-dark.png and for-light.png variants.

### Criterion 3: Market Validation (20% weight)

| Check | Source | Pass | Fail |
|-------|--------|------|------|
| Trend-scout flagged this concept | tresr-trend-scout data | Recent trend signal | No data |
| Keyword volume exists | DataForSEO | 100+ monthly searches | Under 50 |
| Competitors selling similar | Etsy/Amazon search | Active listings with reviews | No listings found |
| Not oversaturated | Competitor count | < 500 competing listings | 1000+ listings |

Score 0-10. This criterion pulls from real data. If data isn't available, score it 5 (neutral) and note "market data pending."

**CRITICAL: Never make up search volumes or competitor counts. Use actual DataForSEO data or mark as pending.**

### Criterion 4: TRESR Brand Fit (15% weight)

Does it match TRESR's identity?

| Check | Pass | Fail |
|-------|------|------|
| Fits a TRESR category | crypto, fitness, coffee, dev, AI, gaming, cats, dogs, meme, entrepreneur, mental health | None of the above |
| Nerdy/techy aesthetic | Has that TRESR vibe | Too mainstream or too niche |
| Works with AI story angle | "An AI designed this" adds to it | AI angle feels forced |
| Not offensive or controversial | Safe for ads | Would get flagged by Meta |

Score 0-10.

### Criterion 5: Historical Similarity (15% weight)

Compare against past performance data.

| Similarity To | Effect |
|--------------|--------|
| Past winners (sub-$1 CPC) | Bonus: +1 to +3 points |
| Past decent performers ($1-$2 CPC) | Neutral: +0 |
| Past losers ($3+ CPC) | Penalty: -1 to -3 points |
| No similar past designs | Neutral: score 5 |

Base score starts at 5, adjusted by similarity to historical data.

### Criterion 6: Technical Quality (10% weight)

| Check | Pass | Fail (Hard) |
|-------|------|-------------|
| Transparent background (alpha channel) | Clean alpha | Solid background |
| No edge artifacts | Clean edges | White/colored fringing |
| Resolution sufficient (min 1024x1024) | Full size | Under 800px |
| File format correct (.png) | PNG | JPG or other |
| Both for-dark and for-light exist | Both present | Missing one |

Score 0-10. Background issues and missing variants are hard fails (score 0).

## Automated Scoring via Gemini Vision

Use the Gemini Vision API to evaluate criteria 1, 2, and 4 automatically.

```javascript
require('dotenv').config({override: true});
const fs = require('fs');
const https = require('https');

const GEMINI_API_KEY = process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY;

async function scoreDesignWithGemini(designPath) {
    const imageBuffer = fs.readFileSync(designPath);
    const base64Image = imageBuffer.toString('base64');

    const prompt = `You are a t-shirt design evaluator for TRESR, an AI-run print-on-demand store.

Evaluate this design for commercial viability as a t-shirt print. Score each criterion 0-10.

CRITERION 1: TEXT READABILITY (would this be readable on a thumbnail?)
- Can you read all text clearly?
- Are there any spelling errors?
- Is the font clean and not overlapping?
- Would this be legible at 200x200 pixels?

CRITERION 2: VISUAL IMPACT
- Does this design have strong contrast?
- Is the composition balanced and centered?
- Is there an immediate visual hook that grabs attention?
- Would this stand out in a social media feed?

CRITERION 4: BRAND FIT (TRESR is nerdy/techy, targets developers, gamers, crypto, AI, coffee lovers)
- Does this fit a clear niche category?
- Does it have a nerdy/techy vibe?
- Would the concept be enhanced by knowing "an AI designed this"?

BUMPER STICKER TEST: Would someone put this on their car? (yes/no/maybe)
TATTOO TEST: Would someone wear this proudly in public? (yes/no/maybe)

Respond in this EXACT JSON format:
{
  "text_readability": { "score": X, "issues": ["issue1", "issue2"] },
  "visual_impact": { "score": X, "notes": "brief assessment" },
  "brand_fit": { "score": X, "category": "best-fit category", "notes": "brief assessment" },
  "bumper_sticker_test": "yes/no/maybe",
  "tattoo_test": "yes/no/maybe",
  "overall_impression": "1-2 sentence honest take"
}`;

    const requestBody = JSON.stringify({
        contents: [{
            parts: [
                { text: prompt },
                {
                    inlineData: {
                        mimeType: 'image/png',
                        data: base64Image
                    }
                }
            ]
        }],
        generationConfig: {
            temperature: 0.2,
            topP: 0.8,
            maxOutputTokens: 1024
        }
    });

    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
        const req = https.request({
            hostname: 'generativelanguage.googleapis.com',
            path: `/v1beta/models/gemini-2.0-flash:generateContent?key=${GEMINI_API_KEY}`,
            method: 'POST',
            headers: {
                'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                'Content-Length': Buffer.byteLength(requestBody)
            }
        }, (res) => {
            let data = '';
            res.on('data', chunk => data += chunk);
            res.on('end', () => {
                try {
                    const response = JSON.parse(data);
                    const text = response.candidates[0].content.parts[0].text;
                    // Extract JSON from response (Gemini sometimes wraps in markdown)
                    const jsonMatch = text.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/);
                    if (jsonMatch) {
                        resolve(JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0]));
                    } else {
                        reject(new Error('Could not parse Gemini response as JSON'));
                    }
                } catch (e) {
                    reject(e);
                }
            });
        });
        req.on('error', reject);
        req.write(requestBody);
        req.end();
    });
}
```

## Design QA Checklist

Reference the 22-point design QA rubric from tresr-design-to-shopify.

### 8 Hard-Fail Checks (any failure = design does not ship)

| # | Check | How to Verify |
|---|-------|---------------|
| 1 | Clean outlines, no gradients (flat color fills only) | Visual inspection |
| 2 | DTF/screenprint ready (max 6 spot colors, no fine details) | Color count check |
| 3 | White background fully removed (no white box artifacts) | Alpha channel check |
| 4 | Text readable on black shirt | View for-dark variant |
| 5 | Text readable on navy shirt | View for-dark variant |
| 6 | Outlines visible on dark shirts (inverted to white) | View for-dark variant |
| 7 | Color accents preserved during inversion (red stays red, not cyan) | Compare variants |
| 8 | Design has enough contrast on white shirt | View for-light variant |

### 14 Quality Checks (scored 1-5, minimum average 3.5)

Transparency, placement, thumbnail legibility, kerning, text-to-art ratio, appeal, style consistency, humor clarity, character consistency, color palette, content crop, no duplicate text, variant completeness, overall polish.

## Composite Score Calculation

```javascript
function calculateCompositeScore(scores) {
    const weights = {
        text_readability: 0.20,
        visual_impact: 0.20,
        market_validation: 0.20,
        brand_fit: 0.15,
        historical_similarity: 0.15,
        technical_quality: 0.10
    };

    let composite = 0;
    for (const [criterion, weight] of Object.entries(weights)) {
        composite += (scores[criterion] || 5) * weight;
    }

    return Math.round(composite * 10) / 10;
}

function getVerdict(score) {
    if (score >= 7.0) return { verdict: 'APPROVED', action: 'Send to tresr-product-launcher' };
    if (score >= 4.0) return { verdict: 'REVIEW', action: 'Flag for human review' };
    return { verdict: 'REJECTED', action: 'Auto-reject, log reason' };
}
```

## Pass/Fail Threshold

| Score | Verdict | Action |
|-------|---------|--------|
| 7.0+ | APPROVED | Proceed to tresr-product-launcher |
| 4.0-6.9 | REVIEW | Flag for human review (Jon decides) |
| < 4.0 | REJECTED | Auto-reject, don't waste ad spend |

**Decision emoji markers:**

| Score Range | Marker | Meaning |
|-------------|--------|---------|
| 8.5-10 | 🏆 | Strong winner candidate |
| 7.0-8.4 | ✅ | Approved, ship it |
| 5.0-6.9 | ⚠️ | Needs review, could go either way |
| 3.0-4.9 | ❌ | Probably not worth testing |
| 0-2.9 | 💀 | Hard fail, don't ship |

## Historical Performance Database

Track every design's predicted score AND actual CPC after ad testing.

### Data Format (JSONL)

```
data/design-scores.jsonl
```

```json
{"slug": "neural-network-coffee", "predicted_score": 8.2, "actual_cpc": 0.45, "category": "developer", "date_scored": "2026-02-13", "date_tested": "2026-02-15", "scores": {"text_readability": 9, "visual_impact": 8, "market_validation": 7, "brand_fit": 9, "historical_similarity": 8, "technical_quality": 9}}
{"slug": "generic-motivational", "predicted_score": 3.1, "actual_cpc": null, "category": "meme-humor", "date_scored": "2026-02-13", "date_tested": null, "scores": {"text_readability": 4, "visual_impact": 2, "market_validation": 3, "brand_fit": 2, "historical_similarity": 5, "technical_quality": 3}, "verdict": "REJECTED"}
```

### Logging Scores

```javascript
const fs = require('fs');

function logDesignScore(slug, scores, compositeScore, verdict, category) {
    const entry = {
        slug,
        predicted_score: compositeScore,
        actual_cpc: null,  // Filled in after ad testing
        category,
        date_scored: new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0],
        date_tested: null,
        scores,
        verdict
    };

    fs.appendFileSync(
        'data/design-scores.jsonl',
        JSON.stringify(entry) + '\n'
    );

    console.log(`Logged: ${slug} = ${compositeScore} (${verdict})`);
}

function updateActualCPC(slug, cpc) {
    // Read all entries, find matching slug, update cpc and date_tested
    const lines = fs.readFileSync('data/design-scores.jsonl', 'utf8')
        .trim().split('\n')
        .map(line => JSON.parse(line));

    const entry = lines.find(e => e.slug === slug && e.actual_cpc === null);
    if (entry) {
        entry.actual_cpc = cpc;
        entry.date_tested = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0];

        fs.writeFileSync(
            'data/design-scores.jsonl',
            lines.map(e => JSON.stringify(e)).join('\n') + '\n'
        );
        console.log(`Updated: ${slug} actual CPC = $${cpc}`);
    }
}
```

## Feedback Loop

Monthly analysis: compare predicted scores vs actual CPC to validate the scoring model.

```javascript
const fs = require('fs');

function analyzeScoringAccuracy() {
    const entries = fs.readFileSync('data/design-scores.jsonl', 'utf8')
        .trim().split('\n')
        .map(line => JSON.parse(line))
        .filter(e => e.actual_cpc !== null);  // Only entries with real CPC data

    if (entries.length < 10) {
        console.log('Not enough data yet. Need 10+ designs with actual CPC.');
        return;
    }

    // Group by score range
    const highScored = entries.filter(e => e.predicted_score >= 7.0);
    const midScored = entries.filter(e => e.predicted_score >= 4.0 && e.predicted_score < 7.0);
    const lowScored = entries.filter(e => e.predicted_score < 4.0);

    const avgCPC = (arr) => {
        if (arr.length === 0) return 'N/A';
        return (arr.reduce((sum, e) => sum + e.actual_cpc, 0) / arr.length).toFixed(2);
    };

    console.log('--- Scoring Model Accuracy ---');
    console.log(`High scored (7+): ${highScored.length} designs, avg CPC: $${avgCPC(highScored)}`);
    console.log(`Mid scored (4-6.9): ${midScored.length} designs, avg CPC: $${avgCPC(midScored)}`);
    console.log(`Low scored (<4): ${lowScored.length} designs, avg CPC: $${avgCPC(lowScored)}`);

    // The model is working if: high-scored designs have lower avg CPC
    const highAvg = parseFloat(avgCPC(highScored));
    const midAvg = parseFloat(avgCPC(midScored));

    if (!isNaN(highAvg) && !isNaN(midAvg)) {
        if (highAvg < midAvg) {
            console.log('Model is WORKING: high-scored designs have lower CPC');
        } else {
            console.log('Model needs ADJUSTMENT: high-scored designs aren\'t outperforming');
        }
    }

    // False negative check: designs scored <4 that actually had sub-$1 CPC
    const falseNegatives = lowScored.filter(e => e.actual_cpc < 1.0);
    const falseNegativeRate = lowScored.length > 0
        ? (falseNegatives.length / lowScored.length * 100).toFixed(1)
        : 'N/A';
    console.log(`False negative rate: ${falseNegativeRate}% (${falseNegatives.length}/${lowScored.length} rejected designs were actually winners)`);

    if (parseFloat(falseNegativeRate) > 5) {
        console.log('WARNING: False negative rate > 5%. Scoring is too aggressive. Consider lowering threshold or adjusting weights.');
    }

    return { highScored, midScored, lowScored, falseNegativeRate };
}
```

### Weight Adjustment

If the feedback loop shows the model isn't predicting well, adjust weights:

```javascript
function suggestWeightAdjustments(entries) {
    // For each criterion, calculate correlation with actual CPC
    const criteria = ['text_readability', 'visual_impact', 'market_validation',
                      'brand_fit', 'historical_similarity', 'technical_quality'];

    for (const criterion of criteria) {
        const withScores = entries.filter(e => e.scores && e.scores[criterion] !== undefined);
        if (withScores.length < 5) continue;

        // Simple correlation: do high criterion scores correlate with low CPC?
        const highCriterion = withScores.filter(e => e.scores[criterion] >= 7);
        const lowCriterion = withScores.filter(e => e.scores[criterion] < 5);

        const avgCpcHigh = highCriterion.reduce((s, e) => s + e.actual_cpc, 0) / highCriterion.length;
        const avgCpcLow = lowCriterion.reduce((s, e) => s + e.actual_cpc, 0) / lowCriterion.length;

        if (avgCpcHigh < avgCpcLow) {
            console.log(`${criterion}: PREDICTIVE (high score = lower CPC). Maybe increase weight.`);
        } else {
            console.log(`${criterion}: NOT predictive (high score doesn't correlate with lower CPC). Maybe decrease weight.`);
        }
    }
}
```

## Full Scoring Pipeline

End-to-end scoring for a single design:

```javascript
require('dotenv').config({override: true});
const fs = require('fs');
const sharp = require('sharp');

async function scoreDesign(slug, category) {
    console.log(`\nScoring: ${slug} (${category})`);
    const scores = {};

    // 1. Technical quality (automated)
    const darkPath = `output/print-ready/${slug}-for-dark.png`;
    const lightPath = `output/print-ready/${slug}-for-light.png`;

    let techScore = 10;
    if (!fs.existsSync(darkPath)) { techScore -= 5; console.log('  Missing for-dark variant'); }
    if (!fs.existsSync(lightPath)) { techScore -= 5; console.log('  Missing for-light variant'); }

    if (fs.existsSync(darkPath)) {
        const meta = await sharp(darkPath).metadata();
        if (!meta.hasAlpha) { techScore -= 3; console.log('  No alpha channel on dark variant'); }
        if (meta.width < 800) { techScore -= 2; console.log(`  Low resolution: ${meta.width}x${meta.height}`); }
    }
    scores.technical_quality = Math.max(0, techScore);

    // 2. Gemini Vision scoring (criteria 1, 2, 4)
    if (fs.existsSync(darkPath)) {
        try {
            const geminiScores = await scoreDesignWithGemini(darkPath);
            scores.text_readability = geminiScores.text_readability?.score || 5;
            scores.visual_impact = geminiScores.visual_impact?.score || 5;
            scores.brand_fit = geminiScores.brand_fit?.score || 5;

            console.log(`  Gemini: readability=${scores.text_readability}, impact=${scores.visual_impact}, fit=${scores.brand_fit}`);
            if (geminiScores.bumper_sticker_test === 'no' && geminiScores.tattoo_test === 'no') {
                console.log('  WARNING: Failed both bumper sticker AND tattoo test');
            }
        } catch (err) {
            console.log(`  Gemini scoring failed: ${err.message}. Using neutral scores.`);
            scores.text_readability = 5;
            scores.visual_impact = 5;
            scores.brand_fit = 5;
        }
    }

    // 3. Market validation (needs real data from DataForSEO/tresr-trend-scout)
    // Mark as pending if no data available
    scores.market_validation = 5;  // Neutral until real data is available
    console.log('  Market validation: scored 5 (pending real data)');

    // 4. Historical similarity
    scores.historical_similarity = calculateHistoricalSimilarity(slug, category);
    console.log(`  Historical similarity: ${scores.historical_similarity}`);

    // 5. Calculate composite
    const composite = calculateCompositeScore(scores);
    const { verdict, action } = getVerdict(composite);

    console.log(`\n  COMPOSITE: ${composite}/10`);
    console.log(`  VERDICT: ${verdict}`);
    console.log(`  ACTION: ${action}`);

    // 6. Log to database
    logDesignScore(slug, scores, composite, verdict, category);

    return { slug, scores, composite, verdict };
}

function calculateHistoricalSimilarity(slug, category) {
    // Check past designs in the same category
    const dataPath = 'data/design-scores.jsonl';
    if (!fs.existsSync(dataPath)) return 5;  // No history yet

    const entries = fs.readFileSync(dataPath, 'utf8')
        .trim().split('\n')
        .filter(line => line.trim())
        .map(line => JSON.parse(line))
        .filter(e => e.category === category && e.actual_cpc !== null);

    if (entries.length === 0) return 5;  // No history for this category

    // Average CPC for this category
    const avgCpc = entries.reduce((sum, e) => sum + e.actual_cpc, 0) / entries.length;

    // If the category generally performs well, give a slight boost
    if (avgCpc < 1.0) return 7;   // Category has winners
    if (avgCpc < 2.0) return 5;   // Category is average
    return 3;                       // Category tends to underperform
}
```

## Batch Scoring

Score all pending designs at once:

```javascript
async function batchScore(designs) {
    // designs = [{ slug: 'my-design', category: 'meme-humor' }, ...]
    const results = { approved: [], review: [], rejected: [] };

    for (const { slug, category } of designs) {
        const result = await scoreDesign(slug, category);

        if (result.verdict === 'APPROVED') results.approved.push(result);
        else if (result.verdict === 'REVIEW') results.review.push(result);
        else results.rejected.push(result);

        // Rate limit Gemini API
        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 2000));
    }

    console.log('\n--- Batch Scoring Summary ---');
    console.log(`Approved (7+): ${results.approved.length} designs`);
    console.log(`Review (4-6.9): ${results.review.length} designs`);
    console.log(`Rejected (<4): ${results.rejected.length} designs`);
    console.log(`Estimated ad spend saved: $${results.rejected.length * 5}`);

    return results;
}
```

## Foundation: ab-test-setup + marketing-psychology (coreyhaines31/marketingskills)

**Experiment design methodology** informs how we validate the scoring model. The scoring system itself is a hypothesis: "designs scoring 7+ will have lower CPC than designs scoring <4." We test this hypothesis monthly against real ad performance data.

**Behavioral science mental models** help predict design appeal:
- Social identity (people wear things that signal group membership)
- Humor as social currency (funny shirts get conversations started)
- Scarcity/uniqueness (AI-designed = nobody else has this exact design)

Our implementation adds the POD-specific lens: we're not scoring "good art" in the abstract. We're scoring "will someone stop scrolling, click an ad, and spend $29 on this?" That's a narrower question with measurable outcomes.

## Scoring Rubric (Meta-scoring: How Well Does the Scorer Work?)

| Metric | Score | Weight |
|--------|-------|--------|
| Designs scoring 7+ have lower avg CPC than 4-6.9 | 0-10 | 35% |
| Pre-filter accuracy (predicted winners match actual) | 0-10 | 30% |
| False negative rate < 5% (don't miss winners) | 0-10 | 20% |
| Scoring model improves monthly (tighter correlation) | 0-10 | 15% |

**The scorer scores itself.** If it's not predicting better than random after 30 days of data, something's wrong with the weights and they need adjusting.

## Integration Points

| Direction | Skill | What |
|-----------|-------|------|
| Receives from | tresr-design-factory | New designs ready for evaluation |
| Sends approved (7+) to | tresr-product-launcher | Designs that passed scoring |
| Sends rejected (<4) to | Review queue | Logged in data/design-scores.jsonl |
| Sends review (4-6.9) to | Human review | Jon decides on borderline cases |
| Feeds accuracy data to | Own scoring weights | Monthly feedback loop |
| Reports to | tresr-weekly-profit-review | Scoring accuracy, filter rate, spend saved |

## Troubleshooting

- **Gemini Vision returns inconsistent scores**: Temperature is set to 0.2 for consistency, but Gemini can still vary. Score the same design 3 times and average if consistency matters for a borderline case.
- **All designs scoring 5-6 (everything is "meh")**: The Gemini prompt might be too cautious. Try adding "Be decisive. A score of 5 means you genuinely can't tell. Use the full 0-10 range." to the prompt.
- **False negative rate too high**: Lower the approval threshold from 7.0 to 6.5 temporarily. Or increase the weight of market_validation (the most objective criterion).
- **No historical data yet**: That's fine for the first month. Historical similarity defaults to 5 (neutral). The model improves as you accumulate data.
- **Market validation always 5**: This criterion needs real DataForSEO data piped in. Until the integration is built, it'll stay neutral. Don't let this hold up the rest of the scoring pipeline.
